reasoning: The Lean signature captures exactly the informal claim. `k ≥ 1` matches the implicit requirement in "$n_1 < \dots < n_k$". The `n : Fin k → ℤ` with `n i > 0` and strict monotonicity encodes the strictly increasing positive integer sequence. The polynomial `1 + ∑ i, z^(n i)` matches $1 + z^{n_1} + \dots + z^{n_k}$ (integer powers of positive integers coincide with natural powers). `Metric.ball 0 ((√5-1)/2)` is the open disk $|z| < (\sqrt{5}-1)/2$, and the conclusion `zpoly z ≠ 0` on this ball exactly says the polynomial has no roots inside that disk. All hypotheses and the conclusion correspond one-to-one with the informal statement.
